item.title: F3-SD: Focal Feature Fusion with Self-distillation on Large Vision-language Models for Cross-modal Retrieval
item.dc:creator: Da Chen
Yangtao Wang
Yanzhao Xie
Xin Tan
Xiaocui Li
Maobin Tang
Meie Fang
Wensheng Zhang
item.description: Recent advances in large vision-language models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance in cross-modal image-text retrieval (ITR) tasks. However, VLMs face critical efficiency-effectiveness trade-offs in ITR, where full-tuning strategies incur prohibitive computational costs while parameter-efficient prompt learning fails to model complex cross-modal correspondences. Through layer-wise analysis, we identify hierarchical semantic dissipation : deeper transformer layers encode discriminative cross-modal representations while shallow layers exhibit underdeveloped alignment capabilities. To address these challenges, we propose F ocal F eature F usion with S elf- d istillation on large VLMs for cross-modal retrieval (termed as F 3 -SD ). Our approach introduces: (1) A self-distillation strategy that transfers knowledge from the final layer to the intermediate key layer (e.g., the 8-th layer), reducing trainable parameters by 80% (e.g., 21.4M (ours) vs. 150M (full-tuning CLIP)) while preserving semantic alignment; (2) A dual-parallel Focal Feature Fusion (F 3 ) module that adaptively combines Top-K significant features with Least-Similar (LS) features through differentiable weighting, leading to focused cross-modal representations. Extensive experiments verify that our F 3 -SD can achieve much higher performance with lower model complexity compared to existing state-of-the-art ITR methods. Take Flickr30K for example, our F 3 -SD has gained more than 13.2% RSUM performance improvement over recently strong baselines, while using 6 × fewer training parameters than conventional fine-tuning. The code of this project is available at GitHub: https://github.com/chd1516/F3SD . Published: 2026-04-02T02:57:18+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.807 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Da Chen; Yangtao Wang; Yanzhao Xie; Xin Tan; Xiaocui Li; Maobin Tang; Meie Fang; Wensheng Zhang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113664

Relevance: 0.807 (must_read)

Recent advances in large vision-language models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance in cross-modal image-text retrieval (ITR) tasks. However, VLMs face critical efficiency-effectiveness trade-offs in ITR, where full-tuning strategies incur prohibitive computational costs while parameter-efficient prompt learning fails to model complex cross-modal correspondences. Through layer-wise analysis, we identify hierarchical semantic dissipation : deeper transformer layers encode discriminative cross-modal representations while shallow layers exhibit underdeveloped alignment capabilities. To address these challenges, we propose F ocal F eature F usion with S elf- d istillation on large VLMs for cross-modal retrieval (termed as F 3 -SD ). Our approach introduces: (1) A self-distillation strategy that transfers knowledge from the final layer to the intermediate key layer (e.g., the 8-th layer), reducing trainable parameters by 80% (e.g., 21.4M (ours) vs. 150M (full-tuning CLIP)) while preserving semantic alignment; (2) A dual-parallel Focal Feature Fusion (F 3 ) module that adaptively combines Top-K significant features with Least-Similar (LS) features through differentiable weighting, leading to focused cross-modal representations. Extensive experiments verify that our F 3 -SD can achieve much higher performance with lower model complexity compared to existing state-of-the-art ITR methods. Take Flickr30K for example, our F 3 -SD has gained more than 13.2% RSUM performance improvement over recently strong baselines, while using 6 × fewer training parameters than conventional fine-tuning. The code of this project is available at GitHub: https://github.com/chd1516/F3SD .

item.title: LG-RobustSAM: A lightweight and robust segmentation model for degraded scenarios
item.dc:creator: Mingxin Chen
Fuwei Wang
Weimin Wang
Xinhao Gao
Zhao Liu
Shenyi Cao
item.description: The Segment Anything Model (SAM) demonstrates strong zero-shot segmentation and generalization, but its heavyweight ViT-H encoder incurs substantial computational and memory overhead, and its performance degrades under image degradations. RobustSAM improves robustness under degraded conditions but still relies on the large ViT-H encoder. In this paper, we propose LG-RobustSAM, a lightweight and robust segmentation model for degraded scenarios. Our approach distills knowledge from the ViT-H encoder into a compact backbone, substantially reducing computational complexity. We further introduce Progressive Robust Prompt Distillation (PRPD), which employs iterative prompting to better model interaction dynamics and improve adaptability under sparse or unseen prompts. In addition, Consistency-Guided Supervision (CGS) alleviates degradation-induced representation shifts and enhances segmentation performance. Under identical settings, LG-RobustSAM reduces parameters by 79%, achieves 15 " role="presentation"> faster inference, and significantly lowers memory usage compared with RobustSAM. Experiments on synthetic and real degradations show that LG-RobustSAM achieves a favorable trade-off among efficiency, robustness, and segmentation accuracy, while outperforming representative lightweight SAM baselines under diverse degraded scenarios. These results suggest that LG-RobustSAM provides an effective lightweight alternative for interactive segmentation in degraded conditions. Published: 2026-04-03T23:53:57+00:00 Venue: Neurocomputing Score: 0.806 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mingxin Chen; Fuwei Wang; Weimin Wang; Xinhao Gao; Zhao Liu; Shenyi Cao

Journal: Neurocomputing

DOI: 10.1016/j.neucom.2026.133556

Relevance: 0.806 (must_read)

The Segment Anything Model (SAM) demonstrates strong zero-shot segmentation and generalization, but its heavyweight ViT-H encoder incurs substantial computational and memory overhead, and its performance degrades under image degradations. RobustSAM improves robustness under degraded conditions but still relies on the large ViT-H encoder. In this paper, we propose LG-RobustSAM, a lightweight and robust segmentation model for degraded scenarios. Our approach distills knowledge from the ViT-H encoder into a compact backbone, substantially reducing computational complexity. We further introduce Progressive Robust Prompt Distillation (PRPD), which employs iterative prompting to better model interaction dynamics and improve adaptability under sparse or unseen prompts. In addition, Consistency-Guided Supervision (CGS) alleviates degradation-induced representation shifts and enhances segmentation performance. Under identical settings, LG-RobustSAM reduces parameters by 79%, achieves 15 " role="presentation"> faster inference, and significantly lowers memory usage compared with RobustSAM. Experiments on synthetic and real degradations show that LG-RobustSAM achieves a favorable trade-off among efficiency, robustness, and segmentation accuracy, while outperforming representative lightweight SAM baselines under diverse degraded scenarios. These results suggest that LG-RobustSAM provides an effective lightweight alternative for interactive segmentation in degraded conditions.

item.title: Window-Free Transformer for Efficient Single Image Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Xue Wu
Kaibing Zhang
Jingwei Xin
Nannan Wang
Xinbo Gao
item.description: Vision Transformer-based models have shown promising performance on various image recovery tasks including image super-resolution (SR). However, their intensive computational complexity hinders the applicability, particularly in resource-limited scenarios. To alleviate this weakness, developing a lightweight yet effective transformer model is worthy of further investigation. In this paper, we elaborate on a novel Window-Free Transformer (WFT) for efficient SR. The WFT consists of three core components, namely multi-head multi-scale spatial self-attention (MMSSA), multi-head channel self-attention (MCSA), and dual spatial-gate feed-forward network (DSGFN). Specifically, the MMSSA achieves long-range dependencies by splitting the attention head into different groups and each group performs spatial self-attention across different scales. The MCSA performs global channel self-attention by measuring attention scores between different channel tokens to strengthen feature interactions across all channels. MMSSA and MCSA can conquer the bottleneck of window-constraint schemes and yield sufficient receptive fields. Additionally, the DSGFN is designed to enhance local contextual information via a dual spatial-gate mechanism. Thorough experimental evaluations demonstrate that the WFT outperforms the existing leading SR methods on five benchmark datasets in both quantitative and qualitative assessments while maintaining a compact model size. Specifically, WFT outperforms SwinIR-light and SRFormer-light by up to 0.38dB and 0.33dB in PSNR on the Urban100 dataset, and by up to 0.41dB and 0.39dB on the Manga109 dataset, respectively, while requiring fewer parameters and computations. Moreover, our lightweight WFT can be scaled to the classical SR task, delivering remarkable SR performance over other competitors. To facilitate reproducibility, we will release the source code upon publication. Published: 2026-04-02T19:51:07+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.803 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xue Wu; Kaibing Zhang; Jingwei Xin; Nannan Wang; Xinbo Gao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3680070

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

Vision Transformer-based models have shown promising performance on various image recovery tasks including image super-resolution (SR). However, their intensive computational complexity hinders the applicability, particularly in resource-limited scenarios. To alleviate this weakness, developing a lightweight yet effective transformer model is worthy of further investigation. In this paper, we elaborate on a novel Window-Free Transformer (WFT) for efficient SR. The WFT consists of three core components, namely multi-head multi-scale spatial self-attention (MMSSA), multi-head channel self-attention (MCSA), and dual spatial-gate feed-forward network (DSGFN). Specifically, the MMSSA achieves long-range dependencies by splitting the attention head into different groups and each group performs spatial self-attention across different scales. The MCSA performs global channel self-attention by measuring attention scores between different channel tokens to strengthen feature interactions across all channels. MMSSA and MCSA can conquer the bottleneck of window-constraint schemes and yield sufficient receptive fields. Additionally, the DSGFN is designed to enhance local contextual information via a dual spatial-gate mechanism. Thorough experimental evaluations demonstrate that the WFT outperforms the existing leading SR methods on five benchmark datasets in both quantitative and qualitative assessments while maintaining a compact model size. Specifically, WFT outperforms SwinIR-light and SRFormer-light by up to 0.38dB and 0.33dB in PSNR on the Urban100 dataset, and by up to 0.41dB and 0.39dB on the Manga109 dataset, respectively, while requiring fewer parameters and computations. Moreover, our lightweight WFT can be scaled to the classical SR task, delivering remarkable SR performance over other competitors. To facilitate reproducibility, we will release the source code upon publication.

item.title: Spectral-Spatial Enhanced Local Contrast Strategy for Hyperspectral Small Air Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Feng Han
He Sun
Lianru Gao
Haoyang Yu
Lulu Qian
Xu Sun
item.description: Detecting small air target is an important task in civil aviation. However, the weak characteristics of these targets make detection challenging. Hyperspectral image (HSI), provides a new approach for the small air target detection task due to its strong ability of capturing both spatial and spectral information simultaneously. In this article, we propose a spectral-spatial enhanced local contrast strategy for hyperspectral small air target detection. An unsupervised band selection step based on the local contrast strategy has been designed based on local contrast (LC-UBSM) to choose bands with better distinguish ability between the target and background in HSI. Then, we have developed an improved RX detection algorithm with combined spatial and spectral variance (CSSV-RX) to detect the target while suppressing both background and noise. Experimental results on both real GAOFEN-5 dataset and simulated dataset based on EO-1 (Earth Observing-1) satellite have validated the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method. Published: 2026-04-02T19:53:25+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.802 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Feng Han; He Sun; Lianru Gao; Haoyang Yu; Lulu Qian; Xu Sun

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3678377

Relevance: 0.802 (must_read)

Detecting small air target is an important task in civil aviation. However, the weak characteristics of these targets make detection challenging. Hyperspectral image (HSI), provides a new approach for the small air target detection task due to its strong ability of capturing both spatial and spectral information simultaneously. In this article, we propose a spectral-spatial enhanced local contrast strategy for hyperspectral small air target detection. An unsupervised band selection step based on the local contrast strategy has been designed based on local contrast (LC-UBSM) to choose bands with better distinguish ability between the target and background in HSI. Then, we have developed an improved RX detection algorithm with combined spatial and spectral variance (CSSV-RX) to detect the target while suppressing both background and noise. Experimental results on both real GAOFEN-5 dataset and simulated dataset based on EO-1 (Earth Observing-1) satellite have validated the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

item.title: Region-Aware MoE Network for Hyperspectral and Multispectral Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Nan Xiao
Xiyou Fu
Qi Ren
Wangquan He
Siqi Wei
Sen Jia
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Existing transformer-based hyperspectral image (HSI) and multispectral image (MSI) fusion methods often suffer from limited cross-modal interaction and insufficient capability to model heterogeneous spatial and spectral patterns across different regions, which restricts their ability to fully exploit modality complementarity. To address these issues, we propose a Region-Aware Mixture of Experts network for HSI and MSI fusion. The proposed framework consists of three key components. First, an attention sharing module (ASM) is designed to explicitly propagate attention information between modalities, enabling more balanced interaction between spatial and spectral representations. Second, the region-aware MoE (RAMoE) is introduced, where multiple region-aware experts (RAEs) capture region-specific feature variations, while an invariant-weight expert (IWE) provides global contextual guidance, improving robustness and generalization. Third, a spatial and spectral reconstruction module (SSRM) is employed to jointly recover spatial details and spectral information through residual learning. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art fusion methods in terms of both quantitative metrics and visual quality. Furthermore, experiments on real-world datasets verify the practical effectiveness of RAMoE in recovering fine spatial details while preserving spectral fidelity. The code will be released at https://github.com/XXn25/RAMoE. Published: 2026-04-02T19:49:56+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.802 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Nan Xiao; Xiyou Fu; Qi Ren; Wangquan He; Siqi Wei; Sen Jia

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3680287

Relevance: 0.802 (must_read)

Existing transformer-based hyperspectral image (HSI) and multispectral image (MSI) fusion methods often suffer from limited cross-modal interaction and insufficient capability to model heterogeneous spatial and spectral patterns across different regions, which restricts their ability to fully exploit modality complementarity. To address these issues, we propose a Region-Aware Mixture of Experts network for HSI and MSI fusion. The proposed framework consists of three key components. First, an attention sharing module (ASM) is designed to explicitly propagate attention information between modalities, enabling more balanced interaction between spatial and spectral representations. Second, the region-aware MoE (RAMoE) is introduced, where multiple region-aware experts (RAEs) capture region-specific feature variations, while an invariant-weight expert (IWE) provides global contextual guidance, improving robustness and generalization. Third, a spatial and spectral reconstruction module (SSRM) is employed to jointly recover spatial details and spectral information through residual learning. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art fusion methods in terms of both quantitative metrics and visual quality. Furthermore, experiments on real-world datasets verify the practical effectiveness of RAMoE in recovering fine spatial details while preserving spectral fidelity. The code will be released at https://github.com/XXn25/RAMoE.

item.title: Rotation-Invariant Representation Learning by Sector Convolution Neural Networks
item.dc:creator: Wenwei Lin
Xunpei Sun
Chonghao Zhong
Haitao Meng
Gang Chen
Bingxian Zhang
Zonghua Gu
item.description: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) demonstrate established performance in challenging vision tasks by leveraging their locally equivariant properties to handle transformations such as shifts, scales, and illumination variations. However, standard CNNs are fundamentally constrained by their fixed square-grid kernels and Cartesian sampling mechanisms. This structural rigidity precludes the intrinsic capture of rotation-equivariant features, inevitably leading to inconsistent predictions when processing identical objects under varying orientations. To address this limitation, we propose RCN, a novel deep learning framework designed to achieve invariance to arbitrary rotations. Rather than relying on rotational data augmentations or rotated square kernels, RCN employs a sector convolution module to enable equivariant feature extraction across all orientations. Additionally, the rotation-invariant encoder, comprising cycling padding and a rotation alignment module, ensures equivariance and aligns feature orientations to produce rotation-invariant representations. Experimental evaluations on rotated image classification, feature matching, and visual odometry tasks confirm the efficacy of RCN, demonstrating its state-of-the-art rotation invariance across all tasks. Published: 2026-04-03T23:48:05+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.801 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Wenwei Lin; Xunpei Sun; Chonghao Zhong; Haitao Meng; Gang Chen; Bingxian Zhang; Zonghua Gu

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113636

Relevance: 0.801 (must_read)

Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) demonstrate established performance in challenging vision tasks by leveraging their locally equivariant properties to handle transformations such as shifts, scales, and illumination variations. However, standard CNNs are fundamentally constrained by their fixed square-grid kernels and Cartesian sampling mechanisms. This structural rigidity precludes the intrinsic capture of rotation-equivariant features, inevitably leading to inconsistent predictions when processing identical objects under varying orientations. To address this limitation, we propose RCN, a novel deep learning framework designed to achieve invariance to arbitrary rotations. Rather than relying on rotational data augmentations or rotated square kernels, RCN employs a sector convolution module to enable equivariant feature extraction across all orientations. Additionally, the rotation-invariant encoder, comprising cycling padding and a rotation alignment module, ensures equivariance and aligns feature orientations to produce rotation-invariant representations. Experimental evaluations on rotated image classification, feature matching, and visual odometry tasks confirm the efficacy of RCN, demonstrating its state-of-the-art rotation invariance across all tasks.

item.title: Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Time Series: A Survey
item.dc:creator: Xin Wu
Fei Teng
Xingwang Li
Ji Zhang
Qiang Duan
Tianrui Li
item.description: Time series frequently manifest distribution shifts, diverse latent features, and non-stationary learning dynamics, particularly in open and evolving environments. These characteristics pose significant challenges for out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization. While substantial progress has been made, a systematic synthesis of advancements remains lacking. To address this gap, we present the comprehensive review of OOD generalization methodologies for time series, organized to delineate the field’s evolutionary trajectory and contemporary research landscape. We organize our analysis across three foundational dimensions: data distribution, representation learning, and OOD evaluation. For each dimension, we present several popular algorithms in detail. Furthermore, we highlight key application scenarios, emphasizing their real-world impact. Finally, we identify persistent challenges and propose future research directions. A detailed summary of the methods reviewed for the generalization of OOD in time series can be accessed at https://tsood-generalization.github.io . Published: 2026-04-03T15:53:22+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.800 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xin Wu; Fei Teng; Xingwang Li; Ji Zhang; Qiang Duan; Tianrui Li

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104336

Relevance: 0.800 (must_read)

Time series frequently manifest distribution shifts, diverse latent features, and non-stationary learning dynamics, particularly in open and evolving environments. These characteristics pose significant challenges for out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization. While substantial progress has been made, a systematic synthesis of advancements remains lacking. To address this gap, we present the comprehensive review of OOD generalization methodologies for time series, organized to delineate the field’s evolutionary trajectory and contemporary research landscape. We organize our analysis across three foundational dimensions: data distribution, representation learning, and OOD evaluation. For each dimension, we present several popular algorithms in detail. Furthermore, we highlight key application scenarios, emphasizing their real-world impact. Finally, we identify persistent challenges and propose future research directions. A detailed summary of the methods reviewed for the generalization of OOD in time series can be accessed at https://tsood-generalization.github.io .

item.title: Rethinking Representations for Cross-Domain Infrared Small Target Detection: A Generalizable Perspective from the Frequency Domain
item.dc:creator: Yimin Fu
Songbo Wang
Feiyan Wu
Jialin Lyu
Zhunga Liu
Michael K. Ng
item.description: The accurate target-background separation in infrared small target detection (IRSTD) highly depends on the discriminability of extracted representations. However, most existing methods are confined to domain-consistent settings, while overlooking whether such discriminability can generalize to unseen domains. In practice, distribution shifts between training and testing data are inevitable due to variations in observational conditions and environmental factors. Meanwhile, the intrinsic indistinctiveness of infrared small targets aggravates overfitting to domain-specific patterns. Consequently, the detection performance of models trained on source domains can be severely degraded when deployed in unseen domains. To address this challenge, we propose a spatial-spectral collaborative perception network (S$^2$CPNet) for cross-domain IRSTD. Moving beyond conventional spatial learning pipelines, we rethink IRSTD representations from a frequency perspective and reveal inconsistencies in spectral phase as the primary manifestation of domain discrepancies. Based on this insight, we develop a phase rectification module (PRM) to derive generalizable target awareness. Then, we employ an orthogonal attention mechanism (OAM) in skip connections to preserve positional information while refining informative representations. Moreover, the bias toward domain-specific patterns is further mitigated through selective style recomposition (SSR). Extensive experiments have been conducted on three IRSTD datasets, and the proposed method consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance under diverse cross-domain settings. Published: 2026-04-02T11:54:28+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.800 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yimin Fu; Songbo Wang; Feiyan Wu; Jialin Lyu; Zhunga Liu; Michael K. Ng

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.800 (must_read)

The accurate target-background separation in infrared small target detection (IRSTD) highly depends on the discriminability of extracted representations. However, most existing methods are confined to domain-consistent settings, while overlooking whether such discriminability can generalize to unseen domains. In practice, distribution shifts between training and testing data are inevitable due to variations in observational conditions and environmental factors. Meanwhile, the intrinsic indistinctiveness of infrared small targets aggravates overfitting to domain-specific patterns. Consequently, the detection performance of models trained on source domains can be severely degraded when deployed in unseen domains. To address this challenge, we propose a spatial-spectral collaborative perception network (S$^2$CPNet) for cross-domain IRSTD. Moving beyond conventional spatial learning pipelines, we rethink IRSTD representations from a frequency perspective and reveal inconsistencies in spectral phase as the primary manifestation of domain discrepancies. Based on this insight, we develop a phase rectification module (PRM) to derive generalizable target awareness. Then, we employ an orthogonal attention mechanism (OAM) in skip connections to preserve positional information while refining informative representations. Moreover, the bias toward domain-specific patterns is further mitigated through selective style recomposition (SSR). Extensive experiments have been conducted on three IRSTD datasets, and the proposed method consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance under diverse cross-domain settings.
